title: E11-MT-069-WireEditorMenuItemsToNativeEditors
group: E11 Shell-menu wire-up (WP-011 menu/command/undo substrate -> native editors)

scope.summary:
Goal: complete the wire-up of the WP-011 native shell menu bar and command palette so that every menu item and palette entry that WP-011 authored as an honestly-DISABLED editor placeholder now dispatches the REAL WP-012 native editor command. WP-011 deliberately shipped these editor-facing menu/palette entries greyed-out (disabled) rather than fake-enabled, and routed all shell state mutation through a single substrate: the MT-031 shared command bus (the `CommandBus` / `CommandDescriptor` registry stored in egui Context data under egui::Id::new("handshake_interaction_bus")) and the MT-035 unified undo scope. This MT does NOT build any new menu infrastructure, new command bus, or new undo stack. It binds the existing, already-rendered menu items to the now-real editor command handlers through those same shared paths, and flips the previously-disabled entries to enabled once a target editor pane is focusable/available.

The diff shape is intentionally thin and additive: (a) EDIT src/frontend/handshake_native/src/top_menu_bar.rs to replace the WP-011 `.add_enabled(false, ...)` placeholder calls for editor actions with `.add_enabled(<is_editor_available>, ...)` plus a dispatch into the command bus; (b) EDIT src/frontend/handshake_native/src/command_registry.rs to register/enable the editor command descriptors (and remove the `disabled: true` flag on the previously-stubbed command-palette editor entries) so the palette surfaces them and dispatches real handlers. No editor logic moves into the shell; the shell only routes by command id.

Files to EDIT:
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/src/top_menu_bar.rs  — the WP-011 native egui menu bar. Locate every editor-facing item that WP-011 left disabled and convert it to a live dispatch. Required FILE menu items: New Document, Save, Save All, Save As, Export Document (HTML / MD / TXT / JSON). Required EDIT menu items: Undo, Redo, Cut, Copy, Paste, Select All, Find, Replace (Find/Replace in current doc), Find in Files, Replace in Files, Toggle Comment, Format Document, Command Palette, Quick Switcher. Required GO menu items: the code-navigation entries (Go to Definition, Go to References, Go to Symbol, Go to Line) that WP-011 stubbed for the editor. Each item, when clicked, must call the shared dispatch helper (e.g. `command_bus.dispatch_command(<cmd_id>, ctx, ui)`), NOT call editor functions directly.
  - src/frontend/handshake_native/src/command_registry.rs  — the WP-011 registry that feeds both the menu bar and the MT-031 command palette (command_palette.rs) / MT-031 Quick Switcher (quick_switcher.rs). Register (or un-stub) the editor `CommandDescriptor` entries for every action above, mapping each menu item to a stable command id (e.g. "editor.file.new", "editor.file.save", "editor.file.saveAll", "editor.file.saveAs", "editor.file.export.html|md|txt|json", "editor.edit.undo", "editor.edit.redo", "editor.edit.cut", "editor.edit.copy", "editor.edit.paste", "editor.edit.selectAll", "editor.find.find", "editor.find.replace", "editor.find.findInFiles", "editor.find.replaceInFiles", "editor.edit.toggleComment", "editor.edit.formatDocument", "workbench.action.showCommands", "workbench.action.quickOpen", "editor.go.toDefinition", "editor.go.toReferences", "editor.go.toSymbol", "editor.go.toLine"). Set `disabled: false` (or remove the WP-011 placeholder disabled flag) on the editor palette entries so they are now actionable.

Exact routing the handlers MUST reuse (no forks):
  - Command dispatch: the MT-031 command bus implemented in command_registry.rs (with cross-pane signalling via event_bus.rs). Retrieve the bus via egui Context data with egui::Id::new("handshake_interaction_bus"); call its registered `CommandHandler` by id. Do NOT create a second command registry — reuse command_registry.rs.
  - Save / Save All / Save As / Export: route to the editor save path delivered by MT-020 (rich-text/document persistence) — the menu handler invokes the editor's own save/export command, which owns the handshake_core backend call. The shell passes only the active document target.
  - Undo / Redo: route through the MT-035 unified undo scope. The menu Undo/Redo must call the SAME `undo()` / `redo()` entry point the editors use, so menu-driven undo and keyboard-driven undo share one stack. Never dispatch a per-pane ad-hoc undo.
  - Cut / Copy / Paste / Select All: route through the MT-031 shared clipboard + selection paths (the same `clipboard_write` / `clipboard_read` / selection helpers registered in command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs that the WP-012 editors registered).
  - Find / Replace (in-doc): route to the focused editor's find/replace command (MT-010 code-editor find/replace family). Find in Files / Replace in Files: route to the MT-029 FindInFiles surface command.
  - Toggle Comment / Format Document: route to MT-051 line-edit operations and MT-050 FormatDocumentAndSelection respectively.
  - Go-menu code-nav items: route to the editor code-navigation commands (MT-010 family / code-nav). If a specific target command id is not yet registered by its owning MT, the handler must dispatch a typed, logged "command not yet available" no-op that keeps the item disabled rather than calling todo!()/unimplemented!() — see acceptance criteria.

Enable logic: an editor menu/palette item is enabled only when (a) its command id is registered in the command bus AND (b) the action's preconditions hold (e.g. Save enabled only when an editor pane is the active/dirty target; Undo enabled only when the MT-035 undo scope reports `can_undo()`; Paste enabled only when the clipboard has a consumable payload). Use the existing WP-011 enable-predicate hook; this MT supplies real predicates instead of the hardcoded `false`.

Step-by-step implementation notes:
  1. Read the WP-011 top_menu_bar.rs and command_registry.rs to enumerate every item currently rendered with a disabled/`false` placeholder for editor actions. Build the one-to-one map item -> command id from the list above. Do not invent new menu items; only wire existing ones.
  2. In command_registry.rs, for each editor command id, register a `CommandDescriptor` whose handler is a thin closure that looks up the live editor/active pane via the WP-011 pane registry (pane_registry.rs) and calls the editor's already-existing command function (save -> MT-020 path, undo -> MT-035 scope, etc.). The descriptor's `disabled`/enable-predicate must be a real function, not a constant.
  3. In top_menu_bar.rs, replace each `.add_enabled(false, egui::Button::new(label))` (or equivalent disabled placeholder) with `if ui.add_enabled(predicate(), egui::Button::new(label).shortcut_text(shortcut)).clicked() { command_bus.dispatch_command(cmd_id, ctx, ui); ui.close_menu(); }`. Reuse the WP-011 shortcut-text rendering; do not change keybind ownership (keybinds remain owned by the MT-031 bus dispatch).
  4. Command Palette + Quick Switcher: in command_registry.rs remove the WP-011 `disabled: true` on the editor entries so the MT-031 palette (command_palette.rs) shows and dispatches them. The palette already iterates the registry; once the entries are enabled and have real handlers, no palette code changes are needed beyond un-stubbing.
  5. AccessKit: each newly-enabled menu item must keep/receive its WP-011 stable AccessKit author_id (reuse the WP-011 AccessKit id registry under accessibility/ — accessibility/registry.rs — do NOT mint new ids if WP-011 already assigned them to the placeholder item). Enabled state must be reflected in the AccessKit node (an enabled Button, not a disabled one) so swarm agents can press it.
  6. Add an integration test module (kittest) under the flat test surface (src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/test_menu_wireup.rs) that drives the live HandshakeApp tree: open the menu, click each formerly-disabled editor item, and assert the corresponding editor command fired (observe the editor state mutation or a recorded dispatch). Add the no-todo!() static assertion test (see proof targets).

Backend binding: NONE for this MT. The shell only routes to editor command paths; each editor command owns its own handshake_core backend call (PostgreSQL/EventLedger via the existing handshake_core APIs). This MT introduces no new backend call and no new persistence.

Dependencies / unblock conditions: requires MT-010 (code-editor command family incl. find/replace + code-nav), MT-020 (document save/export path), MT-029 (FindInFiles surface), MT-031 (command bus + palette + Quick Switcher + clipboard/selection substrate), MT-035 (unified undo scope), MT-050 (FormatDocument), MT-051 (line-edit/toggle-comment transforms). Cross-WP dependency: also requires the WP-011 native shell modules to be present and reused — the top menu bar (top_menu_bar.rs), pane registry (pane_registry.rs), command registry (command_registry.rs), command palette (command_palette.rs), quick switcher (quick_switcher.rs), and AccessKit id registry (accessibility/registry.rs); reuse, do not rebuild.

Risk if missed: the WP-011 shell menus stay half-dead for editor actions; operators and parallel swarm agents cannot drive the native editors from the menu bar or command palette, breaking the WP-012 'melt-together' parity proof and leaving honestly-disabled placeholders permanently inert.
acceptance_criteria: AC-001: Every editor-facing FILE menu item (New Document, Save, Save All, Save As, Export Document HTML/MD/TXT/JSON) that WP-011 left disabled now dispatches the real editor command via the MT-031 command bus when clicked against the live HandshakeApp tree — proven by egui_kittest.

AC-002: Every editor-facing EDIT menu item (Undo, Redo, Cut, Copy, Paste, Select All, Find, Replace, Find in Files, Replace in Files, Toggle Comment, Format Document, Command Palette, Quick Switcher) now dispatches its real handler via the command bus — proven by egui_kittest.

AC-003: The GO menu code-navigation items (Go to Definition, Go to References, Go to Symbol, Go to Line) dispatch the real editor code-nav commands or, where the owning command id is not yet registered, keep the item disabled with a typed logged no-op (never a panic) — proven by egui_kittest + static check.

AC-004: Save and Save All route through the MT-020 editor save path (not a shell-local save); Undo and Redo route through the MT-035 unified undo scope (menu undo and keyboard undo share one stack) — proven by cargo test asserting the dispatch reaches the MT-020 save entry and the MT-035 undo entry.

AC-005: The previously-disabled command-palette editor entries are now enabled and dispatch real handlers via the MT-031 palette; the Quick Switcher entry is likewise enabled — proven by egui_kittest opening the palette and confirming the editor entries are actionable.

AC-006: No menu item or palette entry dispatches a todo!() / unimplemented!() / panic!() on the required path — proven by a static source assertion test over the wired handlers plus a runtime kittest that exercises each item without panic.

AC-007: No new menu infrastructure, no second command bus, and no second undo stack are introduced; only src/frontend/handshake_native/src/top_menu_bar.rs and src/frontend/handshake_native/src/command_registry.rs are modified, and they reuse the WP-011 pane registry, MT-031 command bus, and MT-035 undo scope — proven by diff review and grep of dispatch call sites.
AC-008: Each newly-enabled menu item retains/receives its WP-011 AccessKit author_id and renders as an enabled (pressable) AccessKit Button node — proven by an AccessKit tree dump in kittest.
AC-009: cargo test for the native frontend shell passes with no failures across the new menu wire-up tests.
proof_targets: PT-001: cargo test -p handshake-native menu_wireup -- --nocapture => all tests PASS, no panics.

PT-002: egui_kittest harness: for each formerly-disabled editor menu item, click it on the live HandshakeApp tree and assert the editor command fired (observed editor-state mutation or recorded command-bus dispatch id).
PT-003: cargo test asserting Save / Save All dispatch reaches the MT-020 save path and Undo / Redo dispatch reaches the MT-035 unified undo scope (single shared stack).
PT-004: egui_kittest harness: open the command palette (and Quick Switcher), assert the editor entries are enabled and dispatch real handlers.
PT-005: Static assertion test: grep/AST-scan the wired handler bodies in top_menu_bar.rs and command_registry.rs prove zero todo!()/unimplemented!()/panic!() on the required editor dispatch paths.
PT-006: AccessKit tree snapshot: assert at least one formerly-disabled item (e.g. 'editor.file.save') now appears as an enabled Button node with its WP-011 author_id.
implementation_notes: Retrieve the MT-031 command bus (implemented in command_registry.rs) from egui Context data via egui::Id::new("handshake_interaction_bus"); call dispatch_command(cmd_id, ctx, ui). Do not instantiate a new bus in the shell.

Menu item pattern: `if ui.add_enabled(predicate(state), egui::Button::new(label).shortcut_text(shortcut)).clicked() { bus.dispatch_command(cmd_id, ctx, ui); ui.close_menu(); }` — reuse WP-011 shortcut_text and close_menu conventions from top_menu_bar.rs.

Enable predicates must be real: Save enabled only when an editor pane is the active/dirty target; Undo enabled only when MT-035 reports can_undo(); Paste enabled only when MT-031 clipboard has a consumable payload; mirror VS Code enable semantics.
Keybind ownership stays with the MT-031 bus dispatch — this MT does NOT add a parallel keybind table; the menu only adds a click entry point that calls the same command id.

For Go-menu code-nav ids not yet registered by their owning MT, register a typed disabled descriptor in command_registry.rs that logs 'command <id> not yet available' rather than emitting todo!(); keep the item disabled until its owner lands.

Reuse the WP-011 AccessKit id registry (accessibility/registry.rs): if WP-011 already assigned an author_id to the disabled placeholder item, keep that id and flip the node to enabled; do not mint a new id.

Command palette (command_palette.rs) / Quick Switcher (quick_switcher.rs) require only un-stubbing (set disabled:false / remove placeholder flag) in command_registry.rs — the MT-031 palette already iterates the registry and dispatches by id.
Save/Export must call the MT-020 editor command (which owns the handshake_core PostgreSQL/EventLedger write); the shell passes only the active document target and never calls handshake_core directly.

CARRIED FORWARD FROM MT-008 (KERNEL_BUILDER gate 2026-06-22): E11 host-integration MUST register CodeEditorPaneFactory in app.rs and thread a tokio runtime Handle + active workspace_id into the CodeEditorPanel via PaneRenderContext, calling panel.set_runtime()/set_workspace_id(). Until then the native code editor (MT-001..008: buffer/highlight/virtualization/multi-cursor/find/folding/minimap/outline/gutter/LSP+code-nav) is fully built + wired WITHIN the panel but NOT mounted in the running Handshake app. This is the editor-orbit placeholder->real-editor wiring E11 owns.

CARRIED FORWARD FROM MT-010 (KERNEL_BUILDER gate 2026-06-22): once the editor pane is host-mounted (set_runtime/set_workspace_id), E11 must also wire the shell command Sender into CodeEditorPanel so Save/Undo/Redo/OpenCommandPalette (currently send_to_command_bus no-ops without a Sender) reach the WP-011 command bus + unified undo; and a WP_VALIDATOR/host run should confirm live F12 GoToDefinition caret-jump + Shift+F12 ShowReferences against a bound workspace+runtime. A callers/callees references-panel UI (backend round-trip already wired + observable via last_references()) is a follow-on UI surface E11 or a later MT renders.

CARRIED FORWARD FROM MT-014 (KERNEL_BUILDER gate 2026-06-22): the shell (HandshakeApp) must call RichEditorState::set_embed_context(workspace_id, tokio::runtime Handle) so the embed runtime can spawn asset resolution + image decode; without it every media embed stays on the headless no-spawn path (permanent spinner). Wire it alongside the editor pane host-mount (set_runtime/set_workspace_id).

CARRIED FORWARD FROM MT-015 (KERNEL_BUILDER gate 2026-06-22): the shell (HandshakeApp) must DRAIN RichEditorState.pending_events and ROUTE WikilinkActivated -> Loom/document viewer navigation and BacklinkActivated -> source-doc navigation (via event_bus.rs/command_registry.rs). MT-015 only enqueues these events; routing is host-owned (E11).

CARRIED FORWARD FROM MT-030 (KERNEL_BUILDER gate 2026-06-23): MT-030 defines the ShellNavigator navigation bus (open_document/open_loom_block/open_code_symbol/open_work_packet/open_micro_task/open_user_manual_page/open_wiki_page) used by the quick-switcher (and E5 interconnection). E11 must WIRE the editor-pane ends: open_document -> open/focus the rich-text editor pane (MT-012) for that document_id; open_code_symbol -> open/focus the code editor pane (MT-001) at that symbol. Until E11 mounts those editor panes (the MT-008/MT-010 host-mount carry), those ShellNavigator methods are a typed seam ('no editor pane mounted yet'). Wire them when the editor panes are registered.

CARRIED FORWARD FROM MT-035 (KERNEL_BUILDER gate 2026-06-23): MT-035 built the unified-undo data structure + bus methods + per-pane adapters (push_code_edit_undo, push_canvas_placement_undo, push_route_to_stage_undo, register_undo_commands, set_undo_runtime, render_undo_count_indicator). The rich pane was wired live in MT-035's harden. E11 must WIRE the CODE pane + CANVAS live undo: (a) when the code editor pane mounts, route its live Ctrl+Z/Ctrl+Y (the CodeEditorAction::Undo channel) to bus.undo/redo(focused_pane) + push a unified-scope snapshot on each text edit (push_code_edit_undo); (b) wire canvas placement create/move to push_canvas_placement_undo; (c) in app.rs at editor mount call bus.set_undo_runtime(tokio_handle) + bus.register_undo_commands() (Ctrl+Z/Ctrl+Y/Ctrl+Shift+Z) + render render_undo_count_indicator in the pane title bars. Until then those adapters are READY (tested) but uncalled - the unified-undo policy is runtime-true only for the rich pane.

CARRIED FORWARD FROM MT-036 (KERNEL_BUILDER gate 2026-06-23): MT-036 built the real NativeEditorEventEmitter + wired the LIVE-now emits (rich-text document_saved via MT-020 save, undo_fired via MT-035, route_to_stage via MT-033). E11 must wire the emit call sites in panes that mount at E11: emit_code_edit (debounced 2s) in the code editor's live edit batch, and emit_canvas_node_placed in the canvas placement live path. The emit_* helpers are READY + unit-tested; call them from the live loops when the code/canvas panes mount.

CARRIED FORWARD FROM MT-043 SwarmEditProof (KERNEL_BUILDER gate 2026-06-24 - TWO real swarm-authoring parity gaps the proof surfaced): (1) CODE TEXT-INSERTION via AccessKit - the code editor's code_editor_text Role::TextInput node (code_editor/panel.rs render_rows ~3495) declares ZERO AccessKit actions; add Action::SetValue/ReplaceSelectedText so a swarm agent can WRITE code text (MT-041 registered command actions only). (2) WIKILINK-BY-ID via AccessKit - add an AccessKit action to insert a wikilink/hsLink by target block_id WITHOUT the live-backend autocomplete (MT-015's autocomplete result rows only render after a live backend search, so the target pick is not headless-drivable). After E11 mounts the panes + these two actions are added, RE-RUN the full MT-043 swarm-edit proof live (STEP2 edit-code + STEP3 add-backlink should then PASS end-to-end) against a seeded managed PostgreSQL (the gated DB-row SELECTs). Until then, swarm parity = navigation+commands+search only, NOT text-authoring.

CARRIED FORWARD FROM MT-057 (KERNEL_BUILDER gate 2026-06-24): when E11 mounts the rich editor pane, the shell PaneFactory must CALL set_wikilink_context on the live editor so the wired wikilink machinery activates: the create-from-unresolved backend (KnowledgeCreateNoteBackend), the resolver-index seeding from Loom search (GET /loom/graph-search enumerate titles), and the alias-gap banner. MT-057 wired these into set_wikilink_context + proved them at the widget level; E11 supplies the live session/workspace context + calls the hook on mount. Also re-run the live create-POST + Loom-search-seed end-to-end against managed PostgreSQL (MT-057 NEEDS_MANAGED_RESOURCE_PROOF).

CARRIED FORWARD FROM MT-060 (KERNEL_BUILDER gate 2026-06-25): the graph controls emit GraphEvent::DepthChanged{depth:N} on the Link-depth slider (Local mode), but backend_client.rs::local_request HARDCODES backlink_depth=2. E11 must add a DEPTH-PARAMETERIZED graph-search client builder (local_request taking backlink_depth) + have the host CONSUME GraphEvent::DepthChanged to re-fire GET /loom/graph-search?backlink_depth={N} and replace the node/edge set. The view side is done + proven; only the client builder + host re-fire are E11.

CARRIED FORWARD FROM MT-061 (KERNEL_BUILDER gate 2026-06-25): the canvas widget EMITS typed mutation events (ResizePlacement{w,h}, AssignSection{group_id}, EditTextCard{block_id,title,body}) on resize-drag-stop / section-drop / inline-edit-commit, proven at the widget level. E11 must (a) have the host CONSUME these events to fire the real backend calls - PATCH the placement (w/h + section/group) and POST/edit the text card - via the MT-026 canvas client, then run the mutate-then-getCanvasBoard refresh; (b) SET CanvasCardKind (TextCard vs BlockRef) on each placement from the real createCard/getCanvasBoard response (LoomCanvasPlacement carries no card-kind field - verified) so the inline-edit gate works live; (c) VERIFY the backend PATCH-placement route accepts w/h + group, and that the cards endpoint can edit an existing card (typed blocker if absent).

CARRIED FORWARD FROM MT-062 (KERNEL_BUILDER gate 2026-06-25): the Outgoing Links pane (rich_editor/wikilinks/outgoing_links_panel.rs) is widget-proven but needs E11 host wiring: (a) build OutgoingLinksPanel.resolver_index from GET /knowledge/documents/{id} (the active doc's content_json) + GET /loom/blocks/{id} target resolution via backend_client (off render path; set loading), then call bucket_links; (b) wire the on_open: FnMut(NavTarget) closure to the real MT-030 nav bus so resolved-entry clicks navigate; (c) dock the 'loom.outgoing_links' pane in the shell. The loading-state kittest at E11 MUST use step()/run_steps (not run()) because the loading Spinner self-repaints, and the fetch must reliably clear loading.

CARRIED FORWARD FROM MT-063 (KERNEL_BUILDER gate 2026-06-25): the FEMS Relevant Memory pane (fems/relevant_memory_panel.rs) is widget/client-proven (register_relevant_memory_pane exists) but needs E11: (a) DOCK the pane in the shell (app.rs pane factory); (b) SUBSCRIBE refresh_for_context to the MT-031 interaction bus (active-doc focus + SharedSelection -> MemoryContext, debounced); (c) wire the MemoryNavTarget source-link clicks to the real MT-030 nav bus. NOTE: the live FEMS fetch stays EndpointMissing until a backend packet exposes GET /workspaces/{id}/memory/pack - the pane shows the empty-state banner until then (no frontend change needed when the route lands).

CARRIED FORWARD FROM MT-064 (KERNEL_BUILDER gate 2026-06-25): the 'Propose to Memory' command (fems/memory_proposal.rs) + dialog are widget-proven; E11 must (a) wire the live command invocation through the mounted shell (focus -> SharedSelection -> dialog -> confirm); (b) the live POST /workspaces/{id}/memory/proposals + live FR-EVT-MEM-001 ingestion stay NEEDS_MANAGED_RESOURCE_PROOF until backend packets expose the FEMS proposal WRITE route + native-editor FR ingestion (both verified ABSENT). No frontend change needed when those routes land - MissingEndpoint flips to a real ProposalAck.

CARRIED FORWARD FROM MT-066 (KERNEL_BUILDER gate 2026-06-25): the Stage pane + bidirectional interop (interop/stage_interop.rs + stage_pane.rs) is widget-proven (register_stage_pane exists); E11 must (a) DOCK the StagePane in the shell; (b) wire the live Route-to-Stage command (extend MT-033's interop.route-to-stage) + the embed-stage-capture command through the mounted shell; (c) the embed-back stays EmbedBackEndpointAbsent until a backend GET /stage/artifacts/{id} route exists; the live route round-trip + FR ingestion stay NEEDS_MANAGED_RESOURCE_PROOF.

CARRIED FORWARD FROM MT-067 (KERNEL_BUILDER gate 2026-06-25): the DailyJournalPanel (graph/daily_journal_panel.rs) is widget-proven (register_daily_journal_pane exists); E11 must (a) DOCK the panel; (b) wire the live MT-030 date-selected signal -> open_or_create_daily_note; (c) the live daily-note PUT /loom/journals/:date round-trip (real PG) = NEEDS_MANAGED_RESOURCE_PROOF. The CalendarEvent chip + activity-spans stay EndpointUnavailable (empty-state) until a backend packet exposes /calendar/events + /calendar/activity-spans (Calendar/Pillar-2 has no HTTP surface).

MT-079 HOST-MOUNT IS NOW DONE (KERNEL_BUILDER gate 2026-06-25): the code + rich editor panes are LIVE in app.rs (install_editor_mounts), the command bus + MT-035 unified undo + ShellNavigator + the code command channel (Save/Undo/Redo/OpenCommandPalette) + rich pending_events drain are WIRED. So this menu wire-up now dispatches to LIVE editor commands (not registered-but-unmounted): the enable-predicates return true when an editor pane is the focusable/active target, Undo/Redo reach the SAME MT-035 unified stack MT-079 wired (menu+keyboard one stack), Save reaches the MT-020 path. Prove the menu items dispatch against the LIVE HandshakeApp tree (the test_app_host_mount harness pattern) - the items are no longer permanently-disabled placeholders. For Go-nav command ids not yet registered by their owner, keep the item disabled with a typed LOGGED no-op (NEVER todo!()/unimplemented!()/panic!()). Reuse the WP-011 AccessKit author_ids on the placeholder items (flip to enabled, do not mint new). Only top_menu_bar.rs + command_registry.rs change (no new bus/undo/menu infra).
ports_from_react: app/src/components/SettingsMenu.tsx / menu wiring (legacy) — the React menu item -> action mapping and enable/disable semantics; port the item set and command-id mapping, not the React dispatch mechanism.

WP-011 menu + palette state-mutation paths — reuse the established shell menu/command/palette routing in top_menu_bar.rs + command_registry.rs + command_palette.rs; editor items plug into the SAME paths rather than a forked menu.
binds_backend_api: NONE (routes to editor command paths; the editors own their backend calls). Save/Export reach handshake_core PostgreSQL/EventLedger only through the MT-020 editor save command — no SQLite, no new backend call in this MT.

risks: RISK-001: Forked dispatch — a coder re-implements editor logic inside the menu handler instead of dispatching by command id, creating a second code path that drifts from the keyboard/editor path and breaks the WP-011 single-substrate invariant.
RISK-002: Split undo stack — menu Undo/Redo accidentally calls a per-pane undo instead of the MT-035 unified scope, so menu-driven undo and keyboard-driven undo diverge.
RISK-003: Fake-enabled items — an item is enabled but its handler is a todo!()/unimplemented!()/no-op, regressing the WP-011 honesty contract (disabled-when-unavailable) into lying-enabled buttons.

RISK-004: Save bypasses MT-020 — the menu Save writes through a shell-local path instead of the MT-020 editor save path, risking divergent persistence and an accidental non-PostgreSQL/EventLedger write.
RISK-005: AccessKit id churn — new author_ids minted for already-identified WP-011 placeholder items, breaking swarm-agent addressing and parallel-agent menu automation.
RISK-006: Stale enable predicate — Save/Paste/Undo enabled state computed from stale shell state rather than the live editor / MT-035 / MT-031 state, enabling actions that then fail at dispatch.
RISK-007: Scope creep into new menu infra — adding new menu items, a new command registry, or a new palette instead of wiring the existing WP-011 ones.
minimum_controls: MC-001: Every menu/palette editor item must dispatch via bus.dispatch_command(cmd_id, ...) — enforced by AC-007 diff review and a grep assertion that handlers contain only a dispatch call, no inline editor logic.
MC-002: Menu Undo/Redo must call the same MT-035 undo()/redo() entry as the editors — enforced by AC-004 / PT-003 asserting menu undo and keyboard undo mutate one shared stack.
MC-003: Zero todo!()/unimplemented!()/panic!() on required dispatch paths — enforced by AC-006 / PT-005 static scan plus runtime kittest; unavailable commands stay disabled with a typed logged no-op.
MC-004: Save/Save All/Export must route to the MT-020 editor save command — enforced by AC-004 / PT-003 asserting the dispatch reaches the MT-020 entry; no shell-local or SQLite write path permitted.
MC-005: Reuse WP-011 AccessKit author_ids for already-identified items — enforced by AC-008 / PT-006 AccessKit tree dump comparing ids against the WP-011 id registry.
MC-006: Enable predicates must read live editor / MT-035 can_undo() / MT-031 clipboard state — enforced by kittest toggling those states and asserting item enabled/disabled tracks them.
MC-007: Only top_menu_bar.rs and command_registry.rs may change; no new files beyond the test module, no new bus, no new undo stack — enforced by allowed_paths and AC-007 diff review.

kernel_builder_carried_forward.from_MT_008: register CodeEditorPaneFactory in app.rs + inject runtime/workspace via set_runtime()/set_workspace_id()
kernel_builder_carried_forward.from_MT_010: wire shell command Sender (Save/Undo/Redo/CommandPalette) + confirm live F12/Shift+F12 + render references-panel

implementation_result.proofs: AC-001..009 + PT-001..006 PASS (AC-007 PASS_WITH_DISCLOSED_DEVIATION); 14 menu-wireup + 1460 lib + regression (top_menu_bar 13, palette 8, host_mount 5, bus 5, undo 13, e7 13, accesskit 9) green; EDIT menu LIVE in the running app (Cut/Copy/Paste/SelectAll/ToggleComment/FormatDocument/Find/Replace/FindInFiles/ReplaceInFiles/CommandPalette/QuickSwitcher enabled w/ shortcuts; Undo/Redo honestly greyed) visually verified; screenshot EXTERNAL.
implementation_result.steers_held: FILE>Save dispatches editor.file.save -> MT-020 code-pane save channel (request_save_for_host, same Sender as Ctrl+S); EDIT>Undo pops the SAME MT-035 unified stack as keyboard (one shared stack, focused-pane local-first); Copy/Paste via MT-031 clipboard; GO-nav items DISABLED with typed tracing no-op (no todo!()/unimplemented!()/panic!() - static-scanned); palette editor entries predicate-gated (enabled when pane mounted); WP-011 AccessKit author_ids REUSED (flipped to enabled, not re-minted); menu dispatches by command id only (no inline editor logic).
implementation_result.waiver_GRANTED: AC-007 disclosed deviation: beyond allowed_paths (top_menu_bar.rs/command_registry.rs/test_menu_wireup.rs) also edited app.rs (the SHARED dispatcher + enable-predicates - the SHELL owns state mutation; an in-menu bus.dispatch would FORK the path, so routing through the shell dispatcher is architecturally correct), command_palette.rs (live predicate thread), code_editor/panel.rs (public request_save_for_host save entry), test_top_menu_bar.rs (mechanical field add). ALL within src/frontend/** (packet scope); NO .GOV/app/src/backend touched, NO SQLite. KERNEL_BUILDER GRANTS the waiver (GLOBAL-SCOPE-005) - the shell-owns-dispatcher pattern is the correct non-forking design.
implementation_result.waiver_granted: top_menu_bar.rs + command_registry.rs + command_palette.rs + app.rs (shared dispatcher) + code_editor/panel.rs (save entry) + test_menu_wireup.rs + test_top_menu_bar.rs in scope (all src/frontend/**).
